title: Untitled I
description: A layered composition in terracotta, olive and sky blue. Bold gestural strokes build and dissolve across the surface, the marks accumulating into a landscape of pure feeling.

title: Untitled II
description: Deep greens and earth tones collide with passages of raw pink and burgundy. The painting moves between density and breath, between structure and release.

title: Untitled III
description: Sweeping gestures in black, forest green and grey-white animate the surface. Energy moves across the canvas in rhythm with the artist's own physical momentum.

title: Untitled IV
description: A warm field of ochre and salmon is interrupted by assertive passages of blue and dark brown. Tension and equilibrium held in perfect suspension.

title: Untitled V
description: Loosely interlocking forms in a tightly compressed palette. The mark-making is decisive and immediate, leaving no room for revision.

title: Untitled VI
description: A vertical composition in orange, teal and violet. The painting unfolds from bottom to top in a sequence of interlocking planes that recall both architecture and landscape.

title: Untitled VII
description: Sky blue and chartreuse anchor a composition that opens outward from a dense centre. Movement is the subject and the method simultaneously.

title: Untitled VIII
description: A sparer composition with concentrated energy. The reduced number of marks heightens the presence of each one, making the silence as important as the paint.

title: Untitled IX
description: Warm ochre ground beneath layered passages of cooler tones. The painting reveals itself slowly, each reading uncovering a new spatial relationship.

title: Untitled X
description: A painting of controlled chaos — the marks are free yet the composition holds. An intuitive resolution of opposites.

title: Untitled XI
description: An expansive horizontal movement punctuated by vertical interruptions. The painting feels like a score — rhythm written in pigment.

title: Untitled XII
description: A bridge between Kędzielski's earlier graphic work and his current painting practice. Loose forms suggest the human presence without depicting it.
